meta.title: Nexus Plan — Enterprise forecasting
meta.description: Sales forecasting, revenue planning, scenario modeling, and executive analytics.

dashboard.title: Forecast control tower
dashboard.subtitle: Live P&L, ROI, pipeline coverage, and scenario deltas with instant recalculation across revenue streams and operating leverage.

measures.stripSubtitle: Numbers below are computed once and reused — same engine paths as before, now orchestrated for auditability.
measures.bulbUnifiedBody: We did not replace your math. `evaluateExecutiveWorkspaceMeasures` calls the same `runForecastEngine`, `applyScenario`, `workbook-engine`, and `pipeline` helpers — but returns one snapshot object so dashboards and scenario pages cannot drift apart silently.
measures.bulbTwoRoiBody: Scenario ROI uses monthly P&L revenue from the scenario engine. Workbook ROI uses LOTF sales-target math (fixed ÷ (CM − NP)) and NP at that sales level. They answer different questions; both stay, with clearer ownership in the measure registry.
measures.bulbSalesPlanBody: Sales Plan OS uses `buildSalesPlanModel` (annual SAR, tiers, ADV, funnel). Executive tiles use company + stream demo inputs (monthly). Keys like `salesPlan.revenue.annualSar` vs `exec.revenue.scenarioMonthly` are intentionally separate until actuals unify the grain.

registry.cmBlendedStreams: Simple revenue-weighted average of stream CM%; feeds the P&L scenario path.
registry.revenueBaseline: Monthly revenue from runForecastEngine before scenario levers (uses company.revenueMonthly in the demo workspace).
registry.revenueScenario: Monthly revenue after scenario growth/conversion/mix levers inside applyScenario.
registry.grossProfit: Revenue minus variable costs implied by CM%.
registry.netProfit: Gross profit minus fixed costs (adjusted per scenario).
registry.roiScenario: Net profit divided by fixed costs for the active scenario.
registry.ebitdaScenario: In this build EBITDA equals net profit (simplified demo P&L).
registry.npPctScenario: Net profit divided by scenario revenue.
registry.operatingMarginScenario: Gross profit divided by scenario revenue.
registry.salesTargetScenario: Implied sales target revenue from the scenario engine path (fixed ÷ (CM − target NP) structure).
registry.salesGapScenario: Gap between sales target and current scenario revenue when finite.
registry.burnScenario: Monthly burn when net profit is negative.
registry.cmBlendedWorkbook: LOTF-style blended margin from tier lines and optional block weights (column E).
registry.workbookSalesTarget: Sales revenue needed so that CM × sales − fixed matches NP target structure.
registry.workbookNpAtTarget: NP evaluated at the workbook sales target with the same blended CM.
registry.workbookRoi: NP at sales target ÷ fixed — workbook definition of ROI on fixed.
registry.weightedPipeline: Sum of deal value × probability for the selected company's opportunities.
registry.pipelineHealth: Heuristic score from stages, values, and probabilities.
registry.pipelineCoverage: Weighted pipeline compared to 3× monthly revenue heuristic.
registry.forecastAchievement: Proxy only: scenario revenue vs 105% of baseline monthly plan — replace with actuals + variance later.
registry.salesPlanRevenue: Annual SAR revenue from the wizard model, not the executive monthly tile.
registry.salesPlanAwards: Annual award count implied by annual revenue and portfolio ADV.
registry.salesPlanAdv: Weighted average deal value across active tier cells and service mix.

login.title: Sign in
login.subtitle: JWT session via Supabase Auth. Configure keys to enable authentication.
login.configure: Configure Supabase environment variables to enable auth.

planning.matrixSubtitle: Structured periods with manual drivers and derived KPIs. Changes persist to PostgreSQL when Supabase is configured.
planning.noDatabase: Connect Supabase to enable database persistence and imports.
planning.workbookSubtitle: Replace fragile Excel blocks with typed drivers: tier margins, intra-stream mix, and portfolio weights (column E). Targets recalc instantly using the same formulas as your LOTF model.
planning.blockWeightHint: Share % is the stream portfolio weight (Excel E on the block head). Clear all shares to fall back to global mix-weighted margins.
planning.workbookKpiExplain: Values come from the stream×tier matrix on the Forecast matrix page: blended CM (D16-style), then sales target, NP, and ROI using the same closed-form targets as the spreadsheet.

salesPlan.title: Revenue planning operating system
salesPlan.subtitle: Build a complete sales plan from portfolio definition through opportunity tiers, fixed costs, services, margins, break-even, funnel math, awards (with minimum-deal logic), and quarterly execution — incrementally aligned with your workbook engine.
salesPlan.opportunityTiersIntro: Opportunity tiers replace informal deal-size labels. Each tier carries economics and operational posture used downstream in margin, pipeline, and award math.
salesPlan.tierBandsCompanyHint: SAR bands are stored per company in workspace. Switch company in the shell to load its bands, or edit them under Company management. “Apply to workspace” on this page also saves bands to the selected company.
salesPlan.noWorkspaceCompany: No company is available in the workspace.
salesPlan.noWorkspaceCompanyHint: Reset local workspace storage or refresh the app. Demo data should include at least one company.
salesPlan.noProducts: Add at least one product or service to continue.
salesPlan.cmCascadeNote: Weighted CM follows service portfolio weights × tier mix × tier CM. Override only when you need a board-approved single rate.
salesPlan.awardFloorBody: When the revenue target for a period is below one average deal, the model assumes one award at full ADV so operational planning stays feasible.
salesPlan.awardMismatch: Target revenue is below one average deal: required awards floor to 1 and recognized revenue uses ADV.
salesPlan.quarterlyNote: Quarter weights are normalized for display; edit % then review operational counts per quarter.
salesPlan.dashboardPreviewIntro: Executive charts will consume the same plan object. Below are placeholders for the next iteration (Recharts, tier × service breakdowns).
salesPlan.dashboardLinkHint: Use the Executive page for live charts on the demo workspace while this wizard evolves.

charts.placeholder: Chart wiring lands in a follow-up slice.

operational.note: Per-stream profit bridges (value / cost / profit per tier) extend the workbook formulas and will surface here as the matrix deepens.

tierPurpose.tiny: High velocity, liquidity, and coverage — stabilizes cash conversion even when large deals slip.
tierPurpose.standard: Core operating rhythm; balances margin with predictability for capacity planning.
tierPurpose.big: Margin accretive wins that fund growth investments and cross-sell motions.
tierPurpose.mega: Strategic positioning and lumpy upside — manage concentration risk explicitly in scenarios.

insight.portfolioBody: The portfolio anchors segmentation, capacity, and reporting. All downstream weights and targets inherit this scope boundary.
insight.yearBody: Drives seasonality, quarterization, and year-over-year comparisons. Financial targets are annualized from monthly drivers.
insight.tiersBody: Tiers translate strategy into measurable pipeline and revenue composition. Mix and margins roll up into the same structure as your Excel LOTF blocks.
insight.fixedBody: Fixed costs set the operating hurdle. Break-even and sales-target formulas use the same CM–NP gap as the workbook.
insight.advBody: ADV feeds award requirements. If a target is smaller than one deal, the engine floors to one award — a critical operational truth.
insight.shareBody: You allocate portfolio share before the absolute revenue target is known; the engine resolves SAR targets once CM and NP constraints bind.
insight.cmBody: CM is (Revenue − variable cost) ÷ revenue — not gross margin. It is the correct lever for break-even and NP-target sales because it excludes fixed overhead already modeled separately.

salesPlan.insightsAllClear: No automated risks detected with current assumptions.
salesPlan.applyWorkspace: Apply fixed costs & NP target to workspace
salesPlan.applyWorkspaceHint: Updates the company already selected on the Executive dashboard (fixed monthly, NP target, tier bands).
salesPlan.saveWorkspaceHint: Creates a new company and scenario from this wizard (name = portfolio name) so they appear in Executive / Scenarios dropdowns. Requires portfolio name and at least one service.
salesPlan.saveWorkspaceSuccess: Saved — open Executive dashboard and pick your new company from the list.
salesPlan.saveWorkspaceNeedName: Add a portfolio name (step 1) and at least one service (step 4), then save again.
salesPlan.productDriversCapacityHint: These sliders feed the capacity model (load vs baseline): strategic weight, delivery complexity, and scalability.

insights.serviceShareDrift: Service revenue shares do not sum to 100% — normalize or adjust before committing.
insights.segmentShareDrift: Market segment targets do not sum to 100%.
insights.tierMixDrift: At least one service has tier mix that does not sum to ~100% for active tiers.
insights.megaDependency: Mega-tier weight across the portfolio is high — concentration and execution risk.
insights.quarterOverload: Q4 carries a heavy share of revenue — execution and holiday risk.
insights.npUnreachable: Blended CM is at or below the NP target gap — revenue target becomes infinite or infeasible.
insights.minDealFloor: Some cells use the minimum-deal rule (target revenue below one ADV).
insights.capacityPressure: Operational load index suggests elevated delivery pressure vs modeled capacity.
insights.capacitySevere: Severe capacity stress — financially valid plan may be operationally unsafe.
insights.governmentShareCap: Governmental segment target exceeds a conservative 40% cap — check concentration and procurement cycle risk.

advanced.toggleBulbBody: Turns on an explanatory panel only. It does not replace buildSalesPlanModel or change core math today. Use it to see how the platform can grow toward multidimensional FP&A (dimensions, drivers, actuals) without a rewrite.
advanced.panelIntro: The items below describe the next evolution: same wizard and model, extended incrementally. Nothing here breaks your current flows.
advanced.panelBulbBody: Each card is a future subsystem (like Anaplan/Pigment building blocks). We will wire them behind the same SalesPlanModel and workbook-engine patterns — registry of dimensions, measure graph, assumptions snapshots — rather than bolting on a second app.

dimensionsMeasures.title: Dimensions & measures
dimensionsMeasures.summary: Time, scenario, service, tier, segment, region, department — reusable axes and calculated measures (revenue, CM, NP, ROI, awards, pipeline) with dependency-aware recalculation.
dimensionsMeasures.bulbBody: Today the model already slices revenue by tier and service. A dimension registry formalizes that so every chart and table can pivot consistently and formulas cascade (revenue → profit → ROI) without duplicate spreadsheets.

driversAssumptions.title: Drivers & assumptions center
driversAssumptions.summary: Headcount, bandwidth, utilization, conversion velocity, pricing — operational drivers plus macro knobs (inflation, salary growth, FX) referenced dynamically by all plans.
driversAssumptions.bulbBody: A plan can be financially balanced yet impossible to deliver. Drivers link capacity and hiring to awards so the system can flag “valid on paper, unsafe in execution” before commitment.

actualsGoalsConstraints.title: Actuals, goal-seek & constraints
actualsGoalsConstraints.summary: Track actual revenue/awards/pipeline vs plan, rolling reforecasts, reverse targets (“NP 15% → required revenue”), and guardrails (mega mix, Q4 load, utilization caps).
actualsGoalsConstraints.bulbBody: Some constraint-style checks exist today (mega share, Q4 weight, capacity). This layer generalizes them into a rules engine with violations and suggested fixes — still fed by buildSalesPlanModel outputs.

confidenceSeasonality.title: Confidence & seasonality
confidenceSeasonality.summary: Best / expected / worst cases, confidence scores, optional Monte Carlo; seasonality curves for quarters, procurement cycles, Ramadan or budget windows affecting pipeline timing.
confidenceSeasonality.bulbBody: Executives need ranges, not single points. Seasonality shifts when revenue lands inside the year; confidence layers sit on top of the same drivers without duplicating the core engine.

collaborationInsights.title: Collaboration & smart insights
collaborationInsights.summary: Comments, approvals, audit trail on assumptions; executive narratives (“mega dependency”, “pipeline thin for Q3”) generated from model deltas and driver breaches.
collaborationInsights.bulbBody: Finance, RevOps, and Sales align on one model version. Insights become recommendations tied to data lineage — later backed by persistence and roles, still optional for demo mode.

salesPlan.capacityExplain: Load blends awarded deals with operational complexity and strategic importance; baseline scales with scalability scores.
salesPlan.checklistTitle: Enterprise UX — verified in this build
salesPlan.checkRtl: Locale routing with /en and /ar + RTL document direction
salesPlan.checkCairo: Cairo typography for Arabic via theme
salesPlan.checkCmdk: Command palette (Ctrl/⌘ + K) includes Sales plan OS
salesPlan.checkCharts: Live charts on plan analytics (this step)
salesPlan.roadmapInsight: Additional roadmap items can extend rules and capacity without breaking this release.
salesPlan.roadmap16: More rules: weak CM services, pipeline vs target gap, dependency on single client.
salesPlan.roadmap17: Staffing curves and delivery calendars tied to awards per quarter.
salesPlan.roadmap18: Deeper RTL pass on dense tables and sticky regions.

hrWorkforce.demoSeedDescription: Load a small sample: delivery + corporate roles, an extra department and team, and composed overhead lines so dashboards and intelligence populate immediately. You can edit or delete rows afterward.
hrWorkforce.demoSeedFailed: Could not apply the sample. Ensure there is at least one business unit and department in Organization.
hrWorkforce.dashSubtitle: Executive view of headcount, fully loaded labor rates, overhead rate, and department concentration — isolated from sales planning.
hrWorkforce.dashContextNote: Pick a business unit to drive the whole dashboard: KPIs, charts, lists, and snapshot trend all use that unit’s operational roles. The utilization curve and OH analytics panel use that unit’s OH inputs. Cross-unit comparisons sit in the portfolio section below.
hrWorkforce.dashBuSelectorHint: KPIs, charts, trend, and role lists below follow this unit.
hrWorkforce.dashBuScopeDesc: Headcount, cost, department mix, top roles, OH load, risk list, and snapshot trend are scoped to this unit’s operational roles only.
hrWorkforce.dashPortfolioSectionDesc: Org-wide OH inputs and rates: compare every business unit in one table, then the bar chart when you have more than one unit.
hrWorkforce.dashPortfolioOhChartDesc: Resolved OH $/hr side-by-side for each unit (same engine as Organization → HR & OH).
hrWorkforce.dashChartsDesc: Apache ECharts with rich tooltips. All four panels reflect only the selected unit: department cost, utilization sensitivity for that unit’s OH, top roles by monthly cost in that unit, and snapshot trend of that unit’s monthly workforce cost.
hrWorkforce.dashBuTableDesc: Each unit’s rate, effective billable FTE in the OH denominator, annual billable hours, and numerator — aligned with Organization → HR & OH.
hrWorkforce.kpiMonthlyOhLoadHint: Rough monthly overhead absorbed into labor: for each active role, (fully loaded hourly after OH − standard hourly before that OH layer) × monthly working hours × headcount. Uses each role’s business-unit OH rate; amounts follow your workspace currency (not USD-specific wording).
hrWorkforce.chartEmptyRolesInCharts: No roles to rank yet — add roles under Organization.
hrWorkforce.chartEmptyTrendInCharts: No snapshots yet — save one under Organization to see cost history.
hrWorkforce.kpiHeadcountHint: Sums employee counts on active (non-archived) roles in the selected business unit.
hrWorkforce.kpiBillableVsManual: Manual OH FTE field: {manual}. All billable roles (any structure): {fromRoles}. FTE actually used in OH rate math: {effective} (respects “from roles” vs manual and include-in-OH flags).
hrWorkforce.kpiAnnualRunRate: If this month repeated for 12 months at the same mix and rates, full-year labor spend would be about {v} (same currency as the KPI above).
hrWorkforce.kpiStdHourly: Average standard (pre–OH-surcharge) hourly cost in this unit, weighted by headcount: {v}. The headline figure above adds the unit overhead rate per hour for fully loaded planning.
hrWorkforce.chartDeptCostDesc: Monthly fully loaded cost after risk factor
hrWorkforce.chartUtilSensitivityDesc: Holding overhead and billable headcount constant — not coupled to role-level direct rates.
hrWorkforce.emptyDeptChart: Add roles to see department distribution
hrWorkforce.panelOhAnalyticsDesc: Manual OH methodology inputs drive the rate
hrWorkforce.panelSnapshotsDesc: Monthly workforce cost from saved snapshots (newest first)
hrWorkforce.emptySnapshots: Save a snapshot on the Organization tab to build a trend line
hrWorkforce.listOhImpactDesc: Approx. monthly overhead amount = that row’s unit OH rate per hour × monthly working hours × headcount. Same unit and same FTE often match — ties are sorted A–Z by role name.
hrWorkforce.ohImpactExpandRow: Show OH breakdown for this role
hrWorkforce.rolesSubtitle: Inline edits, duplication, archive, and bulk actions. Calculations refresh from the shared engine.
hrWorkforce.addRoleDisabledHint: No department matches this filter. Add a department under this business unit in Organization → Structure, or pick a department in the filters.
hrWorkforce.roleCostDialogIntro: Per-person averages for this role; headcount on the grid multiplies these in the engine.
hrWorkforce.bulbRoleCostFieldsBody: Currency: ISO 3 letters (e.g. SAR). Salary: monthly gross before Risk %. SI: employer social insurance per month. Annual medical / EOS: per employee per year (engine uses ÷12 monthly). Risk %: planning uplift on subtotal before hourly split (0 = none). Type: employment category for reporting.
hrWorkforce.bulbRoleCostExtraBody: Use for allowances or fees not in the fields above. Fixed / Variable: amount is per employee for each recurrence (the engine multiplies by headcount, same idea as monthly salary). Percentage: amount is a % of the chosen basis (default: monthly salary + benefits stack). Recurrence: Monthly = each month; Yearly = annual ÷12 per employee; One-time = single payment ÷12 per employee for planning.
hrWorkforce.pctBasis_subtotal_before_risk: Payroll stack above + non-% additional (before risk %)
hrWorkforce.pctBasis_loaded_cost: Same as above + all non-% additional lines
hrWorkforce.colBillableHint: Marks client-billable / revenue-facing roles. Used in billable headcount KPIs on the dashboard.
hrWorkforce.colOhAllocHint: When OH billable FTE is set to «from roles», only checked rows are counted in the headcount that drives the overhead rate denominator (with Billable also on).
hrWorkforce.confirmDeleteBU: Delete this business unit and ALL its departments, teams, and roles under it? This cannot be undone.
hrWorkforce.confirmDeleteDept: Delete this department, its teams, and ALL roles in this department? This cannot be undone.
hrWorkforce.confirmDeleteTeam: Delete this team? Roles keep their department; team assignment will be cleared.
hrWorkforce.confirmDeleteRole: Permanently delete this role row? This cannot be undone.
hrWorkforce.confirmDeleteSnapshot: Remove this snapshot from the list?
hrWorkforce.cannotDeleteLastBU: At least one business unit must remain.
hrWorkforce.cannotDeleteLastDept: At least one department must remain.
hrWorkforce.sumActiveTeamsHint: Count of team rows with Active checked under Organization → Structure (all units).
hrWorkforce.bulbRoleTypeBody: Delivery counts toward the OH billable denominator when you derive FTE from roles, and is excluded from the composed indirect payroll pool. Indirect is all non-delivery workforce (admin, support, etc.) for composed annual OH; it does not add to that denominator. Legacy Billable / OH checkboxes stay in sync in storage for imports and older snapshots.
hrWorkforce.kpiDeliveryHint: Selected business unit only. Delivery headcount from roles: {fromRoles}. Billable FTE that enters this unit’s OH rate denominator: {effective}. Manual billable FTE from OH settings when the unit’s FTE source is «manual»: {manual}.
hrWorkforce.bulbDashOhBody: Each business unit has its own OH inputs under Organization → HR & OH. The rate here is for the unit you select; workforce costs use each role’s unit OH rate. When composed OH is on for that unit, the numerator splits into indirect payroll in that unit, non-payroll lines, and the extra annual field.
hrWorkforce.settingsSubtitle: Defaults for costing and manual OH inputs. Monthly working hours use global Weeks/year (not a hardcoded 52).
hrWorkforce.ohSettingsBusinessUnitHint: Manual OH fields and composed lines apply only to this unit. Each unit has its own billable FTE source, utilization, overhead numerator, and non-payroll lines.
hrWorkforce.secOhCalculationDesc: Gross and net capacity are per employee per year from your calendar. Total billable hours multiply net hours by OH utilization and effective billable headcount. OH rate divides the annual OH numerator by those billable hours.
hrWorkforce.ohCalcTotalBillableHint: Net hours × OH utilization × billable employees
hrWorkforce.utilizationRatePctHint: Default is 80% for new workspaces (and for older data we migrate the former “default utilization” here). Change anytime — it drives billable hours in the OH calculation.
hrWorkforce.importSubtitle: Upload a file, map columns, run a dry-run preview (no data changes), then commit. Missing business units, departments, and teams are created only when you import.
hrWorkforce.templateHintBody: Required columns: Business Unit, Department, Team, Role Name, Employment Type, Employee Count, Monthly Salary, Monthly Social Insurance, Annual Medical Insurance, Annual EOS Cost, Risk Factor %, Is Billable, Additional Costs. Additional costs optional cell format: Name:Amount:fixed|variable|percentage:monthly|yearly|one_time — pipe-separated rows.
hrWorkforce.googleSheetsNote: Google Sheets: use File → Download → Microsoft Excel (.xlsx) or CSV, then upload here.
hrWorkforce.bulbPersistBody: Strategy: autosave only (no separate “Save to server” in this module). Zustand persist writes to your browser storage after each change. The timestamp and brief green flash confirm the latest edit was captured locally—use snapshots under Organization if you want named checkpoints you can restore.
hrWorkforce.workspaceSubtitle: The tree mirrors your saved structure. Team depth is optional—turn it off under Organization → Structure if you only use unit → department → roles.
hrWorkforce.bulbTreeBody: Expand a unit to see departments, then teams. Role lines under a team are shortcuts; the main grid is the source of truth for edits. Inactive units/departments/teams still appear but are faded in the engine’s “operational” rollups.
hrWorkforce.bulbTreeBodyFlat: Teams layer is off: each department expands to one shortcut for all roles in that department. Use Organization → Structure to turn teams back on if you later need sub-teams.
hrWorkforce.bulbWorkspaceBody: Archived roles stay hidden unless you enable “Show archived”. Use Role type on each row: Delivery feeds the OH billable denominator when FTE is derived from roles; Indirect covers all non-delivery roles for composed OH. Legacy Billable / OH flags stay synced in storage for imports. Optional annual OH build-up lives under Organization → HR & OH.
hrWorkforce.orgSubtitle: Manage holding-company units, departments, teams, and HR/OH parameters. Tabs separate structure from global numbers so setup stays predictable.
hrWorkforce.bulbOrgBody: Departments always belong to one business unit. Teams belong to one department. Roles reference department (required) and optional team; business unit is stored for fast filtering and should match the department’s unit.
hrWorkforce.hierarchyTitle: How deep is your org chart?
hrWorkforce.useTeamLevelHint: Turn off if you only need Business unit → Department → people. The Teams table and team filters hide so nothing looks “empty”. Turning it on again keeps any teams you already created.
hrWorkforce.bulbHierarchyBody: Many portfolios stop at department. Optional teams stay in data when disabled, but the UI and rollups behave like a two-level org under each unit so you are not forced to invent team names.
hrWorkforce.secBusinessUnitsDesc: Companies, portfolios, or subsidiaries under the holding group.
hrWorkforce.deptTableBuFilterDesc: The table lists only departments for the business unit selected in the dropdown above.
hrWorkforce.deptTableEmptyForBu: No departments for this unit yet — add one with “New department”.
hrWorkforce.bulbOhFteBody: Choose Manual to type billable headcount for OH. Choose From roles to sum employee counts on roles that are not archived and classified as Delivery. The OH engine always uses the resolved count for the denominator. When “from roles” is selected, the billable employees field shows that live derived value (read-only) so it always matches the formula—even though a different manual number may still be stored for when you switch back to Manual.
hrWorkforce.bulbBillableCountUiBody: If the count looked “stuck”, you were probably comparing against the disabled manual value on disk. The displayed number is the live sum from roles; toggling Billable / OH alloc updates it on the next render. KPIs and OH rate use this same resolved value.
hrWorkforce.ohFteDerivedHint: Current derived billable FTE ≈ {n} (used in OH math when source is “from roles”).
hrWorkforce.secOhComposedDesc: Blend workforce-derived indirect labor and named non-payroll lines. Off by default so existing workspaces behave the same.
hrWorkforce.bulbComposedOhBody: When enabled, the annual OH numerator = (annual loaded cost of roles marked not billable, computed at OH rate 0 so it is not circular) + (active lines such as rent or licenses, annualized) + (the numeric field below as an extra catch-all). The OH rate is still total numerator ÷ billable hours. To avoid double-counting salaries, non-billable role rows do not add a second OH surcharge on top of what is already in the numerator; billable roles carry the full OH rate per hour.
hrWorkforce.useComposedAnnualOh: Build annual OH from workforce + lines
hrWorkforce.ohLinesEmpty: No lines yet—add rent, software, legal, etc.
hrWorkforce.totalAnnualOverheadComposedHint: In build-up mode this amount is added after indirect labor and lines. Use 0 if those cover everything.
hrWorkforce.totalAnnualOverheadLegacyHint: Legacy: one total annual OH; OH rate = this total ÷ billable hours.
hrWorkforce.bulbTotalOhBody: Legacy mode: this single number is the full OH numerator. Composed mode: indirect non-billable payroll and your lines are calculated separately; this field is an extra annual bucket for anything else (or zero).
hrWorkforce.importPreviewDesc: Counts of new entities and roles that will be created or updated if you commit. No changes were applied yet.
hrWorkforce.bulbImportBody: Preview runs pure validation and planning in memory—no departments or teams are created until you click Import. That keeps imports predictable and avoids half-applied structure during validation.
hrWorkforce.snapshotRestoreDesc: Replaces the entire in-module structure and roles with the snapshot. Use after saving snapshots you trust.
hrWorkforce.snapshotRestoreConfirm: Replace current workforce data with this snapshot?

intelligence.pageSubtitle: Default is the whole organization. Use the scope control to focus one business unit; KPIs and analytics follow that slice. OH what-if still picks a unit explicitly. Benchmarking always lists every unit for comparison.
intelligence.scopeHint: When a single unit is selected, numbers are limited to roles in that unit’s active structure. Snapshot cost deltas are hidden because snapshots replay org-wide totals.
intelligence.trendHiddenScoped: Snapshot trend is org-wide; switch scope to “All business units” to compare periods on the same basis.
intelligence.kpiMonthlyCostHint: Fully loaded operational payroll per month (active structure, same roll-up as the overview dashboard).
intelligence.kpiHeadcountHint: Sum of employee counts on active roles in the active org structure.
intelligence.kpiDeliveryShareHint: Delivery-class payroll ÷ total payroll — higher usually means more spend is client-facing capacity.
intelligence.kpiOhRateHint: Hours-weighted blend of each unit’s resolved overhead rate.
intelligence.kpiLoadedHourlyHint: Portfolio average fully loaded hourly after OH (headcount-weighted).
intelligence.kpiUtilizationHint: Average of each unit’s OH utilization % weighted by that unit’s delivery billable hours.
intelligence.kpiTop3ConcentrationHint: Share of monthly payroll in the three largest departments — concentration risk signal.
intelligence.kpiDeliveryVsIndirectHcHint: Billable headcount ratio vs non-billable (legacy classification); values above 1 mean more delivery than indirect.
intelligence.orgDistributionDesc: Operational roles only, grouped by business unit, department, and team.
intelligence.orgDistChartEmpty: No department rows to chart yet.
intelligence.orgDistributionBulbBody: Counts follow the same active BU → department → team chain as operational KPIs. Support vs management segmentation is analytics-only and does not change saved role types.
intelligence.orgSpanDesc: Structural load and cost concentration across departments.
intelligence.econBulbBody: Ratios use payroll shares and approximate monthly OH load from hourly surcharges — for executive steering, not statutory accounting.
intelligence.ohCompositionDesc: Approximate monthly slices from composed OH numerators; legacy single-field OH appears in the additional bucket.
intelligence.ohCompositionBulbBody: Non-workforce lines are bucketed by name/category heuristics (rent, software, etc.). Amounts are monthly equivalents of annual inputs.
intelligence.ohCompositionEmpty: No overhead numerator to display — check OH settings per unit.
intelligence.ohScenarioDesc: Simulate changes to utilization, annual overhead field, delivery capacity, and rent-class lines. Does not persist — uses the live OH engine.
intelligence.ohScenarioBulbBody: All levers apply together to the selected business unit. Delivery shrink scales the effective billable FTE used in the OH denominator when sourced from roles or manual count.
intelligence.scenarioUtilDeltaHint: Adds to the unit’s stored utilization percentage before recomputing billable hours (clamped 0–100).
intelligence.scenarioOverheadDeltaHint: Scales the unit’s total annual overhead input (legacy numerator or composed “additional” bucket).
intelligence.scenarioDeliveryDeltaHint: Scales effective billable headcount in the OH denominator (negative = shrink).
intelligence.scenarioRentDeltaHint: Increases or decreases amounts on non-workforce lines classified as rent/lease (composed OH only).
intelligence.capacityDesc: Delivery billable hours and utilization-linked lost capacity.
intelligence.capacityBulbBody: Theoretical hours assume the same headcount at 100% utilization vs stored utilization — a capacity gap proxy, not timesheet actuals.
intelligence.capLostHrsMo: Lost hrs / mo vs full util.
intelligence.benchmarkDesc: Sortable comparison of active units with operational roles.
intelligence.benchmarkOrgWideNote: This table always includes every business unit so you can compare while a unit scope is applied above.
intelligence.benchmarkEmpty: No active units with roles to compare.
intelligence.segmentsDesc: Delivery, support, and management split for analytics.
intelligence.intelAlertIndirectHighBody: Non-billable roles represent a large share of headcount — review span, automation, and shared services.
intelligence.intelAlertDeliveryLowBody: Delivery headcount is a small fraction of the org — capacity to serve revenue may be constrained.
intelligence.intelAlertMgmtHeavyBody: Management segment share is elevated — check layers and decision rights.
intelligence.intelAlertOhLoadBody: Approximate monthly OH surcharge is a large share of payroll — pricing and efficiency deserve attention.
intelligence.intelAlertInactiveStructureBody: Some departments or teams are marked inactive while still present in the tree — clean up to avoid drift.
intelligence.intelAlertCostUpBody: Monthly operational cost rose materially versus the prior saved snapshot.
intelligence.intelAlertCostDownBody: Monthly operational cost fell materially versus the prior saved snapshot.

hrWorkforce.cmpSummary: {a} vs {b}: role rows Δ {roles}, headcount Δ {headcount}, operational monthly cost Δ ≈ {cost} (same engine as dashboard; currency from each snapshot’s settings).
